返回本批次

印度·拉贾布之死 (2024)

Killing of Hind Rajab (2024)

en.wikipedia.org 作者 lr0 分数 55 评论 5 2026/04/12 02:03
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. Hind Rajab 被杀是加沙战争和加沙种族灭绝的一部分拉贾布在高级幼儿园毕业典礼上Tel al-Hawa位置加沙地带位置31°30′49″N 34°26′13″E / 31.5136°N 34.4369°ETTel al-Hawa，加沙地带日期2024年1月29日攻击类型大规模凶杀案、枪杀儿童案；战争罪[1]死亡9名平民肇事者以色列地面部队第401旅（Beni Aharon，根据Hind Rajab基金会）[2]“吸血鬼帝国”连（Major Sean Glass和Itay Choukirkov，根据Hind Rajab基金会）[3]
  2. Hind Rami Iyad Rajab（阿拉伯语：هний рами для руб；[4][5] 2018 年 5 月 3 日至 2024 年 1 月 29 日）是加沙地带的一名五岁巴勒斯坦女孩，在加沙战争期间被以色列国防军 (IDF) 杀害，这场战争还杀死了她的六名家人和两名前来营救的护理人员。拉贾卜和她的家人在逃离加沙城时，他们的车辆遭到以色列国防军的炮击，杀死了她的叔叔、婶婶和三个表兄弟，拉贾卜和另一位表兄弟幸存下来，并联系巴勒斯坦红新月会寻求帮助，同时注意到他们遭到以色列坦克的袭击。后来，她的表弟也被杀了，拉贾布被困在车里几个小时打电话，当时PRCS的护理人员试图营救她。 2 月 10 日，以色列撤军后，拉贾布和医护人员也被发现死亡。以色列声称附近没有任何军队，并否认发动了袭击。然而，《华盛顿邮报》和天空新闻依靠卫星图像和视觉证据进行的调查驳斥了这一说法，调查得出的结论是，确实有一些以色列坦克存在，其中一辆可能向拉贾布及其家人乘坐的汽车发射了 335 发子弹，坦克操作员能够看到车内有包括儿童在内的平民。 [9]法证建筑调查还得出结论，一辆以色列坦克也可能袭击了为拉贾卜而来的救护车。 [9]杀戮事件发生后，西方媒体因其对这一事件的报道而受到批评，包括没有追究杀害拉贾卜的凶手以及将她成人化。 [10][11]美国学生抗议者占领了哥伦比亚大学的汉密尔顿大厅，并以她的名字重新命名为她，这引起了人们对这一事件的更多关注。
  3. 背景 到 2023 年 11 月，即以色列入侵加沙地带大约一个月后，加沙大部分地区遭到严重破坏，大部分地区荒芜并被炸毁。 [12]到 2023 年 12 月，由于以色列的袭击和人道主义援助的封锁，加沙的医疗系统已接近崩溃。 [13]加沙北部受到的影响尤其严重，由于供应品、燃料和人员短缺，截至 12 月该地区还没有一家正常运作的医院。 [14]
  4. 杀人事件 2024 年 1 月 29 日，拉贾布和她的六名家人逃离加沙城 Tel al-Hawa 附近[15][16]，一辆以色列陆军坦克向他们的一辆黑色起亚汽车开火，杀死了拉贾布的姑妈、叔叔和三个表兄弟。[17]另一位幸存者是拉杰布 15 岁的表弟拉扬·哈马德 (Layan Hamadeh)，他向巴勒斯坦红新月会 (PRCS) 寻求紧急援助。哈马德在哭泣，并进一步补充说，“他们向我们开枪。坦克就在我旁边。我们在车里，坦克就在我们旁边。”[18] 哈马德在机枪扫射汽车的声音中惨叫，当时她仍在与救援人员一起排队。[19][20]当调度员回电话时，拉贾布接听了电话，并表示车内其他人都已死亡[16]，并且坦克继续接近汽车。[21]拉杰卜在PRCS的电话里呆了三个小时，她告诉调度员：“我很害怕，请过来。带我走吧。拜托，你会来吗？”[22][16]她的祖父后来告诉记者，拉杰卜的背部、手部和脚部都受伤了。[23] Rajab 被指示继续躲在车内，并准备被 PRCS 救护车救起。 [24]红新月会于 2 月 3 日发布了红新月会、哈马德和拉贾卜之间的电话音频。[23]当该地区被围困时，PRCS 与加沙卫生部和以色列军方合作，确保救护人员营救拉杰布的安全通道。经过几个小时的等待，PRCS 表示已获准将救护车沿着指定路线运送。 [25]救护车报告称，以色列军方正在用激光瞄准他们；据《华盛顿邮报》报道，社交媒体帖子表明，哈马斯过去也使用过这些激光器，但战争研究所的一名研究人员并未观察到冲突双方使用此类激光器的情况。 [26]枪声或爆炸声过后，连接中断。[22][27]拉贾卜和医护人员的命运不得而知[28]，直到 12 天后的 2024 年 2 月 10 日，以色列军队撤军后，一家人返回时，发现车里载着拉贾卜、哈马德和她叔叔的全家其他成员都已死亡。[29]窗户被炸飞，门上布满弹孔。[30]红新月会救护车在几英尺外被发现，已被彻底摧毁[31]，两名救护车工作人员[32]优素福·阿尔-泽诺 (Yusuf al-Zeino) 和艾哈迈德·阿尔-马德霍恩 (Ahmed al-Madhoun) 也被杀害。[33]
  5. 调查 属于拉贾布叔叔的被毁汽车 根据欧洲-地中海人权监测组织的初步调查，拉贾布和她的亲属在一次“有计划的处决”中被以色列军队杀害；以色列国防军还使用美制导弹杀死了派去营救这名年轻女孩的红新月会医护人员。在寻找拉贾卜及其家人的红新月会救护车被炸的现场发现了一枚美国制造的 M830A1 弹丸的弹壳碎片。 [34]美国政府发言人表示，将等待以色列结束对该事件的调查。 [35]以色列国防军发言人表示，车辆附近或射程内没有以色列国防军部队，并且由于缺乏部队，无需协调救护车来营救拉贾卜。 [36]作为对以色列国防军声明的回应，半岛电视台表示，“以色列历来在虐待巴勒斯坦人的情况下迅速清除其部队的任何不当行为。”[37]半岛电视台的一项调查进一步发现，袭击发生时，三辆以色列坦克位于拉贾卜的家庭车辆附近。[38]据报道，调查已移交给总参谋部事实调查评估机制，该机制被描述为调查异常事件的独立军事机构。美国也呼吁及时调查拉贾卜的死因，称他们对拉贾卜的死感到“悲痛”。 [39] PRCS 告诉 The Intercept，以色列军方从未就 Rajab 被杀及其救护车遇袭事件与其联系，驳斥了美国国务院关于 PRCS 和联合国拒绝以色列调查该事件的努力的言论。 [40] 《华盛顿邮报》于 2024 年 4 月进行的一项调查反驳了以色列军方的说法。这项使用卫星图像进行的调查得出的结论是，以色列装甲车就在拉杰布被杀的汽车附近，红新月救护车上的 300 毫米洞与以色列坦克的弹孔一致，尽管它警告说，这只是一种解释，因为“关于手工生产的哈马斯弹药的数据很少”。 [26] 《邮报》还证实，救护车残骸是在 COGAT 提供的路线上发现的，COGAT 是以色列国防部的一个部门，负责与以色列国防军协调医疗车辆的安全通道。 [26]天空新闻 2024 年 10 月援引简斯信息服务部专家的话说，救护车的损坏与被“大口径武器”击中相符。报告指出，虽然以色列国防军表示事发时并不在该地区，但它可能无意中反驳了这一说法，发布了一份有关其在沙蒂和特尔哈瓦地区行动的新闻稿，该新闻稿后来从其网站上删除。天空新闻的进一步调查显示，1 月 29 日（袭击当天）拍摄的卫星图像显示，Tel al-Hawa 街区至少有 15 辆军车，其中最近的车辆距离救护车袭击地点仅 300 米。 [25] Forensic Architecture 于 2024 年 6 月公布了其调查结果，依靠视觉、音频和其他收集到的证据来重建该事件。调查得出的结论是，一辆以色列坦克可能向 Rajab 及其家人乘坐的汽车发射了 335 发子弹，坦克操作员能够看到车内有包括儿童在内的平民。它还得出结论，以色列坦克也可能袭击了前来援助拉杰布的救护车。 [9]联合国人权理事会于 2024 年 7 月任命的独立专家表示，拉贾卜的杀戮可能构成战争罪。 [1]拉贾卜死后不久，巴勒斯坦外交部呼吁国际刑事法院（ICC）追究那些对她的死亡负有责任的人的责任。 [41] 2024年2月13日，人权组织“人人正义”向国际刑事法院提交案件，指控以色列国防军因杀害拉杰卜而犯有多项战争罪。[42][43] 2025 年 5 月 3 日，即欣德的七岁生日，欣德·拉贾布基金会声称已确认杀害欣德的营长是第 401 装甲旅的贝尼·阿哈龙中校，并向国际刑事法院提出了针对他的战争罪申诉。 [44][2]

Original Summary

  1. Killing of Hind RajabPart of the Gaza war and the Gaza genocideRajab at her graduation ceremony for senior kindergartenTel al-HawaLocation within the Gaza StripLocation31°30′49″N 34°26′13″E / 31.5136°N 34.4369°ETel al-Hawa, Gaza StripDate29 January 2024Attack typeMass homicide, child homicide by gunshot; war crime[1]Deaths9 civiliansPerpetrators Israeli Ground Forces 401st Brigade (Beni Aharon, per Hind Rajab Foundation)[2] "Vampire Empire" company (Major Sean Glass and Itay Choukirkov, per Hind Rajab Foundation)[3]
  2. Hind Rami Iyad Rajab (Arabic: هند رامي إياد رجب;[4][5] 3 May 2018 – 29 January 2024) was a five-year-old[a] Palestinian girl in the Gaza Strip who was killed by the Israel Defense Forces (IDF) during the Gaza war, which also killed six of her family members and two paramedics coming to her rescue. Rajab and her family were fleeing Gaza City when their vehicle was shelled by the IDF, killing her uncle, aunt and three cousins, with Rajab and another cousin surviving and contacting the Palestine Red Crescent Society (PRCS) to ask for help while noting that they were being attacked by an Israeli tank. The cousin was later also killed and Rajab was left stranded in the vehicle for hours on the phone, as paramedics from PRCS attempted to rescue her. Both Rajab and the paramedics were later also found dead on 10 February after an Israeli withdrawal. Israel claimed that there were not any troops present in the neighborhood and denied carrying out the attack. However, this was refuted by The Washington Post and Sky News's investigations relying on satellite imagery and visual evidence, which concluded that a number of Israeli tanks were indeed present and one had likely fired 335 rounds on the car that Rajab and her family had been in, with tank operators being able to see that the car had civilians including children in it.[9] The Forensic Architecture investigation also concluded that an Israeli tank had also likely attacked the ambulance that came for Rajab.[9] In the aftermath of the killing, Western media outlets were criticized for their coverage of the incident, including for not attributing who killed Rajab and for their adultification of her.[10][11] American student protestors occupied and renamed Hamilton Hall in her honor at Columbia University, drawing increased attention to the incident.
  3. Background By November 2023, about a month after the Israeli invasion of the Gaza Strip, much of Gaza was heavily damaged and largely deserted and bombed-out.[12] By December 2023, the healthcare system in Gaza was close to collapse due to Israeli attacks and a blockade of humanitarian aid.[13] Northern Gaza was particularly affected, as there were no functioning hospitals in the area by December due to shortage of supplies, fuel and staff.[14]
  4. Killing On 29 January 2024, Rajab along with six of her family members were fleeing from the Gaza City neighborhood of Tel al-Hawa,[15][16] when an Israeli army tank shot their vehicle, a black Kia, killing Rajab's aunt, uncle, and three cousins.[17] The only other survivor, Rajab's 15-year-old cousin Layan Hamadeh, called the Palestine Red Crescent Society (PRCS) for emergency aid. Hamadeh was crying, further adding that "They are shooting at us. The tank is right next to me. We're in the car, the tank is right next to us."[18] Hamadeh was heard screaming as she was killed under the sound of machine gun fire raking the car while still on the line with responders.[19][20] When the dispatchers called back, Rajab answered the call, stating everyone else in the car was dead[16] and that the tank continued to approach the car.[21] Rajab stayed on the line with the PRCS for three hours, telling the dispatcher, "I'm so scared, please come. Come take me. Please, will you come?"[22][16] Her grandfather later told reporters that Rajab was injured in the back, hand and foot.[23] Rajab, who was instructed to continue hiding in the vehicle, was set to be rescued by a PRCS ambulance.[24] The audio of the phone call between the PRCS, Hamadeh and Rajab was published by the Red Crescent on 3 February.[23] As the area was besieged, the PRCS worked with the Gaza Health Ministry and the Israeli military to guarantee safe passage for their ambulance crew to rescue Rajab. After hours of waiting, the PRCS says it was given the approval to send the ambulance along a designated route.[25] The ambulance reported that they were being targeted by the Israeli military with lasers; according to the Washington Post, social media posts suggest that these lasers have in the past also been used by Hamas, but that a researcher at the Institute for the Study of War had not observed the use of such lasers by either side in the conflict.[26] After sounds of gunfire or an explosion, the connection was lost.[22][27] The fate of Rajab and the paramedics was unknown[28] until 12 days later on 10 February 2024, when the family returned after the withdrawal of the Israeli military, discovering the car with Rajab, Hamadeh, and the rest of her uncle's entire family deceased.[29] The windows were blown out, and the doors were covered with bullet holes.[30] The Red Crescent ambulance was found a few feet away, completely destroyed[31] with two ambulance workers,[32] Yusuf al-Zeino and Ahmed al-Madhoun, also killed.[33]
  5. Investigation The destroyed car that belonged to Rajab's uncle According to an initial investigation from the Euro-Mediterranean Human Rights Monitor, Rajab and her relatives were killed by the Israeli army in a "planned execution"; using a US-made missile, the IDF also killed the Red Crescent paramedics sent to rescue the young girl. Shell fragments of an American-made M830A1 projectile were found at the site of the bombed Red Crescent ambulance that was looking for Rajab and her family.[34] A spokesperson for the U.S. government said that it would wait for Israel to conclude its investigation of the incident.[35] An IDF spokesperson stated there were no IDF troops near the vehicle or within firing range and that, due to the lack of troops, ambulance coordination was unnecessary to rescue Rajab.[36] In response to the IDF statements, Al Jazeera stated, "Israel has a history of swiftly clearing its troops of any wrongdoing in cases of abuse against Palestinians."[37] An Al Jazeera investigation further found that three Israeli tanks were in the vicinity of Rajab's family vehicle at the time of the attack.[38] The investigation was reportedly handed over to the General Staff Fact Finding Assessment Mechanism, described as an independent military body that investigates unusual incidents. The United States has also raised calls for Rajab's death to be investigated in a timely manner, stating they were "devastated" about Rajab's death.[39] PRCS told The Intercept that the Israeli military never contacted it regarding the killing of Rajab and the attack on its ambulances, refuting the State Department's remarks that PRCS and the United Nations had rebuffed Israeli efforts to investigate the incident.[40] An investigation conducted by The Washington Post in April 2024 countered claims made by the Israeli military. The investigation, which used satellite imagery, concluded that Israeli armored vehicles were in the vicinity of the car where Rajab was killed, and the 300mm hole on the Red Crescent ambulance is consistent with an Israeli tank round, although it cautioned that this was just one explanation, as "there is little data on craft-produced Hamas munitions".[26] The Post also confirmed that the wreckage of the ambulance was found on a route provided by COGAT, an arm of the Israeli Defense Ministry that coordinates safe passages for medical vehicles with the IDF.[26] Sky News reported in October 2024 that the damage to the ambulance was consistent with having been hit by a "large calibre weapon", citing an expert from Janes Information Services. The report indicated that while the IDF said it was not in the area at the time of the incident, it may have inadvertently contradicted this assertion by publishing a press release about its operations in the Shati and Tel al-Hawa neighborhoods, which was later deleted from its website. Further investigation by Sky News revealed satellite imagery taken on 29 January, the day of the attack, showing at least 15 military vehicles in the Tel al-Hawa neighborhood, with the closest vehicle located just 300 meters from the site of the ambulance attack.[25] Forensic Architecture publicized its investigation in June 2024, which relied on visual, audio and other collected evidence, to reconstruct the event. The investigation concluded that an Israeli tank had likely fired 335 rounds on the car that Rajab and her family had been in, and that tank operators would have been able to see that the car had civilians including children in it. It also concluded that an Israeli tank had also likely attacked the ambulance that came to the aid of Rajab.[9] Independent experts appointed in July 2024 by the United Nations Human Rights Council stated Rajab's killing might amount to a war crime.[1] Shortly after Rajab's death, the Palestinian Foreign Ministry called on the International Criminal Court (ICC) to hold accountable those responsible for her death.[41] On 13 February 2024, the human rights group Justice For All submitted a case to the ICC charging the IDF with multiple war crimes for the killing of Rajab.[42][43] On 3 May 2025, which would have been Hind's seventh birthday, the Hind Rajab Foundation claimed that it had identified the commander of the battalion that had killed Hind as Lieutenant Colonel Beni Aharon of the 401st Armored Brigade, and filed a war crimes complaint against him with the ICC.[44][2]

评论（默认中文）

khaledh 2026/04/12 03:12
让我们记住，这不是一个孤立的事件，这是以色列国防军故意针对和杀害平民和援助人员的重复模式：

- 面粉屠杀

- 世界中央厨房无人机袭击

- 加沙援助物资分发大屠杀

- 拉法医护人员大屠杀

还有许多其他人。其中每一项都是战争罪。但不幸的是，西方乐于对此视而不见。如果情况相反，我们永远不会听到它的结局。

Let's remember that this is not an isolated incident, it's a repeated pattern of the IDF intentionally targeting and killing civilians and aid workers:

- Flour massacre

- World Central Kitchen drone strike

- Gaza aid distribution massacres

- Rafah paramedics massacre

And many others. Each one of these alone is a war crime. But unfortunately the west is happy to look the other way. Had it been the other way around, we'd never hear the end of it.

在 HN 中查看
arvid-lind 2026/04/12 07:32
有一段时间每天醒来，读到以色列国防军如何再次等待饥饿的巴勒斯坦人在他们认为可以获得人道主义援助的地方排队并向全体人群开枪，这真是太可怕了。

人们认为这是某种圣战的想法真是令人作呕。

It was horrific waking up every day there for a while and reading about how the IDF had, yet again, waited for starving Palestinians to line up at places they speculated would have humanitarian aid and opened fire on the whole crowd.

The idea that people think this is some kind of holy war is something beyond nauseating.

在 HN 中查看
OutOfHere 2026/04/12 07:42
我认为我们作为一个民族必须继续努力削弱宗教身份并用对私人神的信仰取代宗教身份。对神力的信仰不能离开自己的家，也不能延伸到公共领域，以免导致分裂。这就是多神教获胜的地方，因为它们允许存在一个私人上帝，同时又与其他人的私人上帝完全兼容。如果我能通过一个人的服装或发型猜出他的宗教信仰，那就意味着侵犯了隐私原则，并播下了分裂的种子。

Fwiw，黎凡特地区的古埃及宗教是多神教。许多古代宗教都是多神教，因此更加灵活、分散和多元化。相比之下，一神论在很大程度上是不灵活的，并且有可能以宗教名义引发冲突。

至于耶和华的信徒，即现在所谓的犹太教，三千年来他们一直在使用暴力取代其他形式的多神教迦南宗教。现在发生的事情几乎是一样的。甚至在两千多年前，犹太人就向罗马人施加压力，要求他们将基督钉在十字架上。关键是一神论者对固有的宗教多样性是零容忍的。

一神教的类比是每个人都崇拜美元，而多神教则是人们有更多的选择，甚至是多种选择。

I think we as a people have to keep working to weaken and replace religious identity with belief in a private god. Belief in a divine power must not leave one's home, and must not extend into the public sphere where it can lead to divisions. This is where polytheistic religions win because they allow for a private god while being entirely compatible with someone else's private god. If I can look at a person's clothing or hairstyle and guess their religion, it means the tenet of privacy is violated, and division is sowed.

Fwiw, ancient Egyptian religion in the Levant region was polytheistic. So many ancient religions were polytheistic, thereby more flexible, decentralized, and pluralistic. Monotheism in contrast is largely inflexible and risks breeding conflict in the name of religion.

As for the adherents of Yahweh, i.e. now called Judaism, they have been using violence to displace other forms of the polytheistic Canaanite religion for three thousand years. What is happening now is just more of the same. Even two thousand years ago, the Jews pressured the Romans to crucify Christ. The point is that there is zero tolerance among monotheists for innate religious diversity.

An analogy for monotheism is everyone worshipping the dollar, whereas polytheism is whereby people have more choice, even multiple choices.

在 HN 中查看
khaledh 2026/04/12 11:16
不管你相信与否，犹太人和基督徒在伊斯兰黄金时代自由地信奉他们的宗教并受到保护。我们今天看到的是（任何​​宗教中的）极端分子上升到权力位置（或拿起武器），从而扭曲了这样一种观点：这就是该宗教的所有追随者的信仰。实际情况比这要微妙得多。只要受到尊重并且权利不被侵犯，大多数人都会和平共处，乐于共处。如果去掉这一点，极端主义就会抬头。
Believe it or not, jews and christians were practicing their religion freely and were protected during the Islamic golden age. What we're seeing today is extremists (in any religion) rising to position of power (or taking up arms), thus skewing the view that this is how all followers of that religion believe. It's much more nuanced than that. Most people are peaceful and happy to coexist as long as they're respected and their rights are not violated. Take that away and extremisim will rise.

spwa4 2026/04/12 05:59
如果巴勒斯坦政府还呼吁国际刑事法院对 https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidnapping_and_killing_of_the_... ... 毕竟，他们清楚地知道谁应该为绑架、折磨并最终杀害一名母亲和两个孩子（其中一个还是婴儿）负责。

顺便说一句，这场“纪念”活动的幕后黑手 Dyab Abou Jahjah 因声称自己回到黎巴嫩“帮助屠杀基督徒和犹太人”而在比利时出名（他的话，不是我的，只是翻译的）

在这里，他花了整场辩论来证明谋杀是正当的，因为“人们都很忙”。他正在做你经常看到的穆斯林所做的事情：他混淆了关于真主党的每一个事实（真主党是在“抵抗”以色列，而不是向村庄发射火箭。它没有伊朗的支持。真主党只是在保卫无辜的黎巴嫩人，有时甚至是巴勒斯坦人（如果你不知道：真主党在黎巴嫩屠杀了巴勒斯坦人）。真主党并没有为了支持阿萨德而屠杀叙利亚人。它没有进行屠杀库尔德人并没有屠杀抗议伊朗人，而且以色列的袭击绝对不是真主党行动的直接结果，整个黎巴嫩，包括大多数真主党成员，都因此而憎恨他们）

是的，在你问之前，我认为观众中没有人不知道这个人在撒谎。

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0M7_oTcIMbY

This would be 1000x more impressive, or should I say believable, if the Palestinian government had ALSO called for ICC action on https://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Kidnapping_and_killing_of_the_... ... After all, they know exactly who is to blame for kidnapping, torturing, and eventually killing a mother and 2 children, one of them a baby.

And Dyab Abou Jahjah, the person behind this "remembrance" effort, by the way, is famous in Belgium for claiming he went back to Lebanon to "help massacre Christians and Jews" (his words, not mine, just translated)

Here he is spending an entire debate justifying murder because "people are occupied". He is doing what you frankly often see muslims do: he obfuscates every fact about Hezbollah (it is "resisting" Israel, not firing rockets at villages. It does not have Iranian support. Hezbollah is merely defending innocent Lebanese, and sometimes Palestinians (if you don't know: Hezbollah massacred Palestinians in Lebanon). Hezbollah did not go massacre Syrians in support of Assad. It did not go massacre Kurds and it did not go massacre protesting Iranians. And it is absolutely not the case that Israel's attacking was the direct result of Hezbollah's actions and all of Lebanon, including most of Hezbollah's own members, hate them for it)

And yes, before you ask, I don't think anyone in the audience is unaware this person is lying.

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=0M7_oTcIMbY
